data.title: Turning Fans Into Stakeholders: An Exclusive Interview with Giggle Co-Founder Cindy
data.description:
An Exclusive Interview with Cindy Wu, Co-Founder of Giggle, an AI- and Web3-powered platform reimagining fan engagement through a new "Interact-to-Earn" model. Backed by 3 Body Labs, Giggle transforms fan interactions into tokenized, investable assets—ushering in a new era of interactive IP. Wu shares insights into the platform, its implications for creators and communities, and what's next for this emerging industry.

SILICON VALLEY, CA / ACCESS Newswire / May 9, 2025 / From Play-to-Earn to Move-to-Earn, the "X-to-Earn" wave has already reshaped gaming and fitness. Now a new frontier is emerging: Interact-to-Earn, where fan engagement itself becomes an investable asset. Giggle, an AI- and Web3-powered platform backed by 3 Body Labs, wants to make that future real. We sat down with Co-Founder Cindy to unpack how Giggle tokenizes fan interaction, why "interactive IP" is the next big category, and what's coming next.

Cindy Wu, Co-Founder of Giggle (2025)

Interview with Giggle's Co-founder, Cindy Wu:

Interviewer: Cindy, in one sentence, what is Giggle?

Cindy: Giggle is the monetization infrastructure for interactive IP - we let creators and IP holders turn fan actions (remix, vote, duet, meme) into on-chain value with IP Tokens.

Interviewer: Where did the idea originate?

Cindy: My co-founder Roger and I noticed two gaps:

  • Fans already remix everything on TikTok and YouTube but capture zero upside. Studios struggle to quantify that engagement in P&L terms.

  • Giggle bridges both by issuing IP Tokens and sharing revenue every time a fan sparks interaction.

Interviewer: Walk us through the core product stack.

Cindy:

  • AI Content Engine - generates derivative videos, artwork, even music stems in minutes.

  • Engagement Widgets - plug-and-play "Vote", "Remix", "Duet" modules.

  • IP Token Issuer - one-click smart-contract deployment with compliance presets (US/EU/Asia).

  • DEX Liquidity Router - auto-routes fees and buybacks to keep tokens liquid.

Interviewer: How does Interact-to-Earn work in practice?

Cindy: Fans use a widget - say an AI Remix challenge. Each submission mints an on-chain proof. Smart contracts send token rewards to the fan, royalties to the IP owner, and a slice to a buy-back pool that supports price stability. Everyone wins.

Interviewer: You're incubated by 3 Body Labs, known for an AI film-generation stack. How does that synergy play out?

Cindy: Giggle plugs directly into 3 Body Labs' AI Director, so an influencer can drop a 30-second vocal stem and get a full-blown music video fans can remix within hours - no studio overhead.

Interviewer: What traction can you share?

Cindy:

  • Pilot IPs signed to date

    • A U S. competition franchise with 50 million+ fans

    • A globally renowned sci-fi IP already adapted into a Netflix series (season 2 now in production)

    • A Billboard-charting singer

    • A leading film studio in India

    • A Hong Kong film studio controlling a library of 600+ classic HK movies

  • On-chain performance (private-beta stage)

    • The first two Hong Kong movie IP tokens issued on Giggle now average US $100 K+ in daily trading volume

    • Combined fully diluted market cap has surpassed US $3 million

Interviewer: Roadmap for the next six months?

Cindy:

  • Launch Interact-to-Earn pilot with 10 flagship IPs.

  • Open Widget Store to third-party devs.

  • List the first batch of IP Tokens on major DEXs.

  • Release our creator dashboard with real-time royalty analytics.

Interviewer: One call-to-action for IP owners or influencers reading this?

Cindy: If you have a community that loves to remix or meme your work, DM us. Early partners get rev-share upside and governance tokens in the Giggle ecosystem.

Closing

Giggle's bet is simple: the more interactive a piece of IP becomes, the more valuable it is. By letting fans earn alongside creators, the company hopes to rewrite the economics of fandom.

For more information about Giggle, please visit app.giggle.pro.

Giggle: The Future of IP (2025)

About Giggle

Giggle is a Silicon Valley-based IP Tokenization and Monetization infrastructure platform designed for content creators, studios, and rights holders across film, television, music, sports, and digital media. Its proprietary plug-in and dashboard allow clients to tokenize any video content as an IP Token, which can be bought, traded, or licensed directly on their own platforms. Clients can then create unique AI-based experiences through built-in applications to engage with their fans.

Giggle

228 Hamilton Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301

info@giggle.pro

1-669-236-1344

SOURCE: 3 Body Labs

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Lyft Price Target Raised to $17.00/Share From $15.00 by Oppenheimer
Grain Traders Look Ahead to WASDE — Market Talk
data.description: CBOT grain futures have pared gains in preparation for Monday's WASDE. The report is highly anticipated, with traders eager to see early forecasts for grains as spring planting progresses. Traders will also look for information on tariffs and grain demand. "It will be interesting to see whether the USDA takes into account the effects of the tariff conflict on Chinese imports and U.S. exports," says Commerzbank in a note. "Soybeans in particular would be affected by this." Soybeans are up 0.9%, but corn and wheat are now losing ground, with corn falling 0.3% and wheat down 1.3%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Onto Innovation Is Maintained at Outperform by Oppenheimer
Top Midday Gainers
data.description:
Nuvve Holding said Friday that it "engaged multiple" digital asset advisory consultants to expedite the growth of its newly formed subsidiary, Nuvve-DigitalAssets.

Shares more than doubled as intraday trading volume soared to over 207.5 million from a daily average of about 1.2 million.

Gogo shares soared 49% amid heavy trading after the company reported Friday that Q1 revenue more than doubled from a year ago.

Intraday trading volume surged to more than 8.9 million shares from a daily average of about 1.6 million.

Microchip Technology reported fiscal Q4 non-GAAP earnings and sales above expectations late Thursday and issued a fiscal Q1 outlook that also topped consensus.

Shares jumped 11% as intraday trading volume advanced to more than 13.2 million from a daily average of about 12.1 million.

data.title: YourUpdateTV speaks with Marisa Brahney: GUILT-FREE GIFTING FOR MOTHER’S DAY
data.description:
Emmy-Award Winning Television Host and Lifestyle Expert, Marisa Brahney, Shares Her List of Must-Haves for Mother’s Day

Marisa Brahney, Emmy-Award Winning Television Host and Lifestyle Expert partners with DS Simon Media, Affirm, Mediheal, SHEIN, and Vitamin Shoppe

NEW YORK, May 09, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) —

Mother’s Day is all about helping the moms in our lives look and feel their best without overspending. Emmy Award-winning television host and lifestyle expert Marisa Brahney is sharing her top guilt-free gifting picks across fashion, finance, skincare, and wellness—making it easier than ever to smartly spoil Mom this season.

STYLE THAT SPEAKS: ROMANTIC FLORALS & EFFORTLESS LOOKS

This Mother’s Day is all about soft pastels, flattering florals, and chic comfort. SHEIN is a go-to online destination for trend-forward fashion and accessories that are easy on the eyes and the wallet.

Discover Marisa’s favorite picks from three SHEIN trend stores: Emery Rose, Luneh, and Missguided.:

  • Emery Rose Floral Print Ruffle Hem Smock Dress – feminine floral design with a collared neckline, ruffle hem, and playful silhouette in green or orange.
  • Luneh Slogan Letter Printed Mom Sweater – The perfect cozy, laid-back comfort with a loose fit, rib-knit details, and casual slogan style in curve sizing.
  • Missguided Lounge Set – Effortless long-sleeved, button-down top and wide-leg pants in four neutral tones.

Shop now at SHEIN.com or via the SHEIN app and search #treatmom or the trend stores for affordable, curated finds.

Direct link: https://us.shein.com/ark/4536

Social Media Handles:

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/SHEINUS/

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/shein_us/?ref=fb&hl=af

A SMARTER WAY TO PAY FOR THE PERFECT GIFTS

U.S. consumers are expected to spend $34.1 billion this Mother’s Day, with average spending projected at $259 per person. Affirm offers a flexible way to pay over time, with no late or hidden fees for eligible consumers.

“From headphones to a new dress or the sunglasses she’s been eyeing, Affirm offers a smarter way to pay — so you can treat your mom to something special,” says Brahney.

Shop at retailers like Amazon, The RealReal, StockX, Net-a-Porter, and many more. Download the Affirm app or select Affirm at checkout to get started.

Direct link: www.affirm.com

Social Media Handles:

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/AffirmInc/

Instagram: http://www.instagram.com/affirm

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/affirm

X https://x.com/Affirm?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Eauthor

SKINCARE THAT WORKS: BOOST SKIN ELASTICITY & REDUCE FINE LINES

Next on Marisa’s list? A glow-up for Mom, courtesy of Mediheal, Korea’s leading derma-cosmetic brand trusted by millions.

Top Mediheal must-haves for Mother’s Day:

  • Collagen Ampoule Pads – Boost skin elasticity by 106% and reduce fine lines by up to 33%. These dual-use toner/mask pads are infused with collagen, ceramides, and milk protein for a nourishing anti-aging treatment.
  • Retinol Collagen Eye Masks – Formulated with 95% pure retinol and a 5-layer collagen complex, these eye masks depuff, brighten, and target 10 types of wrinkles.

Perfect for skincare beginners and beauty buffs alike, Mediheal offers professional-level results with the ease of an at-home spa day.

Direct link: Mediheal on Amazon

Social Media Handles:

Instagram: @mediheal_global

TikTok: @mediheal_global

Twitter (X): @medihealus

THE GIFT OF CALM: WAYS TO UNWIND NATURALLY

Finally, when it comes to feeling her best, self-care starts from within. Brahney recommends Solgar’s Magnesium Glycinate capsules and Magnesium Citrate powder, available at The Vitamin Shoppe, to help Mom unwind naturally.

  • Supports relaxation, bone health, and cellular energy
  • Highly bioavailable, Vegan-friendly, easy-to-absorb formulas
  • Available in gentle capsules or smooth, mixable powders

Now, it’s easier to help moms create a wellness ritual that they’re thank you for all year long.

Find Solgar products in-store or at vitaminshoppe.com. America’s destination for trusted supplements and holistic wellness solutions.

data.title: Potential Sequence of Trade Deals Could Boost Sentiment — Market Talk
data.description:
1350 ET - The U.S.-U.K. trade deal is "one step on a longer journey towards certainty," DWS's George Catrambone says. Now, "heads will turn towards India, Vietnam, Switzerland, Japan. Hopefully those are the next dominoes to fall," he says. As trade deals materialize, "you can start to get some momentum there, that will build certainty." Catrambone says a sequence of such agreements could help improve sentiment after the hectic start of Trump's trade policy reshuffle, although it could take a while until the full impact of tariffs is reflected in hard data. "It is not so easy to tell you what the effect of one deal or more would be on growth and inflation," he says. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1314 ET - Canada's April employment report falls on the dovish side of the scale yet continues nothing especially decisive, strategists at TD Securities reckon. Robert Both and Andrew Kelvin say the soft data should help nudge the Bank of Canada toward another quarter percentage point interest rate cut in June, notwithstanding the inherent volatility in Canadian jobs data. But they believe the central bank will still want to see more evidence of weaker sentiment translating into softer GDP growth before officials step off the sidelines. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1312 ET - Mexico's industrial production likely fell in March from the previous month, returning some of February's strong manufacturing-led gains, while rising from a year earlier on a favorable calendar effect. Industrial production is seen down 1.5% from February in seasonally adjusted terms, according to the median estimate of analysts surveyed by WSJ. Production is expected to be up 1.4% unadjusted from March of 2024, benefiting from this year's shift in the Easter holiday to April. Statistics institute Inegi is scheduled to report March industrial production on Monday. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1249 ET - Canada's April jobs report continues the weakening trend from the month before and supports expectations interest rates will fall another quarter percentage point at the next Bank of Canada meeting in June, says Citi's Gisela Young. Young notes the details of the report show meaningful weakness in manufacturing, with the sharpest drop in employment for the sector since 2020 and a third decline in a row. The economist expects the labor force survey to show more weak readings in the coming months, particularly in May with slower hiring in the private sector amid an uncertain environment and when the temporary increase in public administration employment linked to the recent federal election fades. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1157 ET - Fixed-income investors face a challenging outlook as lawmakers look poised to widen the federal budget shortfall, Harbor Capital's Jake Schurmeier says. He is worried the U.S. debt will increase without a proportional boost to economic growth, while trade tensions push the world away from the dollar. "You have this kind of lower growth, higher-friction U.S. economy combined with a large, unfunded broadly non-stimulating fiscal package," he says. "I don't want to be holding a lot of U.S. fixed income in that environment." Instead, Schurmeier likes diversifiers including gold and non-U.S. developed-economy stocks. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1142 ET - The dollar retains its safe-haven status even as President Trump's widespread trade tariffs have led investors to diversify away from U.S. assets, says Blake Gwinn, head of U.S. rates strategy at RBC Capital Markets. "If you need liquidity in a time of stress you need dollar liquidity. There is no deeper or more liquid market," he says. Alternatives such as gold, European bonds or Japanese bonds could never provide the liquidity U.S. Treasurys and the dollar provide, he adds. Still, Gwinn says the U.S. has recently "lost some of its stability premium." The trend of 'de-dollarization' is worth watching but it is a longer-term story where the impact is likely to be marginal, he says. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

1055 ET - Short-dated U.S. Treasurys and U.K. gilts are more attractive than their long-dated peers due to expectations of further interest-rate cuts by the U.S. Federal Reserve and the Bank of England, Richard Flax, chief investment officer at Moneyfarm says in a note. Short-dated government bonds offer attractive yields and continued interest-rate cuts are likely to boost their performance versus long-dated bonds, Flax says. Markets fully price in two more interest rate cuts of 25 basis points each by both the U.S. Fed and the BOE by year end, LSEG data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

1052 ET - U.S. rate-cut expectations and investors moving away from the dollar mean the U.S. Treasury yield curve should steepen as short-dated yields fall by more than their longer-dated peers, says Blake Gwinn, head of U.S. rates strategy at RBC Capital Markets. "In the near term, the two-year yield will incorporate [expectations of] more rate cuts," he says. Longer-dated yields will fall by less as rate cuts ensure longer-term economic stability. Meanwhile, the recent 'de-dollarization' trend, where tariff worries prompt investors to seek alternatives to U.S. assets, should also steepen the yield curve, Gwinn says. De-dollarization will mean investors likely avoid long-dated Treasurys, he says. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

1026 ET - This is going to be a particularly tough summer for Canadian graduates to land a job, says RSM Canada's Tu Nguyen. The assurance, tax and consultancy firm's economist says April job number showcase the impact of tariffs on the country's economy: a mere 7,400 jobs were added and the jobless rate rose 0.2 percentage point to 6.9%. Nguyen notes a stark contrast between goods and services sectors, with goods producers losing 33,000 jobs, almost all in manufacturing, but services producers adding 40,300 jobs. She anticipates further job less in the coming months, creeping the unemployment rate up to 7% as job loss spread across industries including travel, real estate and entertainment. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1023 ET - Canada's jobs data for April marks the "first direct hit" from the US-Canada trade conflict, and highlight the negative turn underway for the Canadian economy unless trade tensions cool, says Brendon Bernard, Canadian economist for Indeed.com, the job-search site. March's jobs report indicated a decline in employment but Bernard says the previous-month losses weren't led by trade-exposed sectors. The big drops in April were recorded in manufacturing, wholesaling and retail trade, and natural resources. Bernard says with the unemployment rate now approaching 7%, more months like April would send the labor market to its weakest state in years, excluding the Covid-19 pandemic. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

0950 ET - Bank of Canada should be worried about April's employment report, says Desjardins Group economist Tiago Figueiredo. He estimates that without hiring tied to the April 28 federal election, employment in Canada likely would have dropped by up to 30,000. The unemployment rate rose to 6.9%, a post-pandemic high. April marks the third straight month of weak jobs data, and Figueiredo says he expects BOC officials to consider a quarter- or half-point rate cut in June to offer support for economy. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

0947 ET - Canada's April jobs data were dismal, even with the temporary lift provided by election-related jobs, says Doug Porter, chief economist at BMO Capital Markets. The 7,400 net jobs gain in month masks some startling figures, Porter says. Chief among them, he says, is a decline of nearly 31,000 positions in manufacturing--a drop akin to what the sector sustained during the pandemic and the 2008-09 global financial crisis. "It doesn't take an archeological dig to realize this is a weak report," Porter tells clients, adding average hourly wage gains have slowed to a 3-year low. He says the jobs data "clearly increases" the likelihood of a quarter-point Bank of Canada rate cut in June. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

data.title: Bulgaria's president to propose referendum on euro adoption in 2026
data.description:
SOFIA (Bulgaria), May 9 (SeeNews) - Bulgaria's president Rumen Radev will propose to parliament to hold a referendum on whether the country should adopt the euro in 2026, as planned by the government, the head of state said on Friday.

"There is no consensus in Bulgarian society neither about our readiness for euro adoption, nor about the date," Radev said in an address to the nation published on the presidency website.

Radev spoke less than a month before the European Central Bank and the European Commission are expected to issue extraordinary convergence reports on the country’s readiness to join the eurozone in 2026. The reports are expected to be positive as Bulgaria, a member of the EU since 2007, has met all the Maastricht criteria for eurozone membership.

Inflation, lack of trust in politicians and the absence of clear steps by the those in power to protect people's purchasing power and the competitiveness of Bulgaria's economy are creating tensions, Radev added. The introduction of the single European currency should be based on a convincing national consensus, he concluded.

Radev's proposal for a referendum echoes calls by the pro-Russia anti-EU Vazrazhdane party, the third largest formation in parliament. In February, ten people were arrested for attacking the building of the European Commission in Sofia during a protest organised by Vazrazhdane.

Last month, Fitch ratings agency affirmed Bulgaria's long-term foreign-currency issuer default rating at 'BBB' with a positive outlook, reflecting the country's prospects for euro adoption.

data.title: National Bank on NFI's Q1
data.description:
NFI's first-quarter results were modestly ahead of estimates, notes BMO. While seat supply challenges persist, green shoots are emerging with a new supplier expected to ramp deliveries through H2/25, analyst John Gibson writes.

Gibson has lifted the price target on NFI by $1, to $14, but elevated leverage and potential downside from upcoming tariffs remain concerns. Reiterate Market Perform rating

data.title: Shareholders who lost money on Civitas Resources, Inc. (CIVI) Should Contact Wolf Haldenstein
data.description:
NEW YORK, May 09, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Wolf Haldenstein Adler Freeman & Herz LLP, a distinguished law firm with over 125 years of history, informs investors that a class action lawsuit has been filed against Civitas Resources, Inc. ("Civitas " or the "Company") on behalf of a class consisting of all investors that purchased or otherwise acquired Civitas securities between February 27, 2024 and February 24, 2025, both dates inclusive (the "Class Period").

Deadline to sign up:

If you are an investor who bought or otherwise acquired Civitas securities during the Class Period, you have until July 1, 2025, to ask the Court to appoint you as Lead Plaintiff for the class.

PLEASE CLICK HERE TO JOIN THE CASE AND SUBMIT CONTACT INFORMATION

What happened:

The filed complaint alleges that throughout the Class Period, the defendants made materially false, misleading and potentially damaging statements regarding the Business Operations of Civitas. These include unvalidated claims about the Company's status, capabilities, and prospects, which have had considerable adverse effects on investors who relied on the information shared.

On February 24, 2025, Civitas announced its Q4 and full-year 2024 financial results, which included revenue of $1.29 billion, missing consensus estimates by $3.44 million, and non-GAAP earnings per share of $1.78 for the quarter, missing consensus estimates by $0.21 per share. In addition, Civitas reported net income of $151.1 million, or $1.57 per share, compared with $302.9 million, or $3.23 per share, in the year-ago quarter.

Additionally, on February 24, Civitas outlined the Company's 2025 outlook, which noted that, compared to Q4 2024, "lower volumes are primarily driven by the DJ Basin, due to natural declines following peak production in the fourth quarter." In addition, Civitas announced a 10% reduction in its workforce across all levels.

Finally, the Company announced the termination of its Chief Operating Officer Hodge Walker and Chief Transformation Officer Jerome Kelly.

On this news, Civitas's stock price fell $8.95 per share, or 18%, to close at $40.35 per share on February 25, 2025

Why Wolf Haldenstein Adler Freeman & Herz LLP?:

This illustrious firm, founded in 1888, is steadfast in their pursuit of justice for investors who have suffered financial harm due to these misrepresented statements. The law firm brings to the fore over 125 years of legal expertise in securities litigation and has a proven track record of protecting the rights of investors.

We encourage all investors who have been affected or have information that will assist in our investigation, to contact Wolf Haldenstein Adler Freeman & Herz LLP.

Contact:

  • Phone: (800) 575-0735 or (212) 545-4774
  • Email: classmember@whafh.com
  • Contact Person: Gregory Stone, Director of Case and Financial Analysis

Firm Website: Wolf Haldenstein Adler Freeman & Herz LLP

data.title: Princeton Bancorp, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Princeton Bancorp, Inc., a prominent financial institution, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report showcases the company's robust financial performance and strategic business operations, reflecting its resilience and growth in a dynamic economic environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest and Dividend Income: $33.3 million, an increase of $5.2 million compared to the same period in 2024, driven by higher loan balances and yields.
  • Net Interest Income: $18.8 million, up by $3.3 million from the first quarter of 2024, primarily due to higher interest income on loans and securities.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: $268 thousand, reflecting an increase of $82 thousand compared to the first quarter of 2024.
  • Total Non-Interest Income: $2.2 million, an increase of $205 thousand or 10.3% from the prior year period.
  • Total Non-Interest Expense: $13.8 million, increased by $2.0 million or 16.5% due to higher salaries, data processing, and other expenses.
  • Net Income: $5.4 million, an increase from $4.3 million in the first quarter of 2024, primarily due to higher net interest income.
  • Earnings per Common Share - Diluted: $0.77, up from $0.68 in the first quarter of 2024, reflecting improved profitability.

Business Highlights

  • Geographical Performance: The commercial real estate portfolio is distributed across New York (45.3%), New Jersey (39.1%), and Pennsylvania (14.1%), with a small portion in other regions. The New York market remains the largest segment, although it saw a slight decrease in its share compared to the previous period.
  • Commercial Real Estate Portfolio: The CRE portfolio includes multi-family, owner-occupied, and non-owner-occupied properties. Multi-family properties constitute 37.4% of the portfolio, with a weighted average loan-to-value (LTV) of 53.0%. Owner-occupied properties make up 29.3% with a 36.2% LTV, while non-owner-occupied properties, including retail and office buildings, account for 31.3% of the portfolio.
  • Loan Portfolio Growth: The loan portfolio saw a net increase of $37.7 million, driven by a $29.2 million rise in residential mortgages and a $19.0 million increase in commercial real estate loans. This growth was partially offset by decreases in construction loans and commercial and industrial loans.
  • Non-Performing Assets: Non-performing assets decreased by $614 thousand, representing 1.43% of total loans, net of deferred fees and costs, as of March 31, 2025. This marks an improvement from the previous period.
  • Deposit Trends: Total deposits decreased by $22.0 million, with significant reductions in money market deposits and certificates of deposit. However, there was an increase in interest-bearing demand deposits and savings deposits.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued management of interest rate risk and credit risk, with a focus on maintaining a strong capital position. The economic environment remains uncertain, with potential impacts from geopolitical tensions and inflationary pressures.

SEC Filing: Princeton Bancorp, Inc. [ BPRN ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: NABORS INDUSTRIES LTD SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Nabors Industries Ltd, a leading provider of advanced drilling solutions for the oil and gas industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, reflecting the impact of recent acquisitions and market conditions on its business segments.

Financial Highlights

  • Operating Revenues: $736.2 million, representing a decrease of $2.5 million compared to the three months ended March 31, 2024.
  • Net Income Attributable to Nabors: $33.0 million, a $67.3 million increase from a net loss of $34.3 million in the prior year period, primarily due to a gain on bargain purchase related to the Parker acquisition.
  • Net Income Per Diluted Share: $2.18, compared to a net loss of $4.54 per diluted share for the three months ended March 31, 2024.
  • General and Administrative Expenses: $68.5 million, an increase of $6.8 million or 11%, reflecting increases in workforce costs and general operating costs due to the Parker acquisition and inflationary pressures.
  • Depreciation and Amortization Expense: $154.6 million, a decrease of $3.0 million due to older assets reaching the end of their useful lives.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments:
    • The U.S. Drilling segment experienced a 15% decrease in operating revenues due to a 13% decrease in the average number of rigs working, while pricing remained stable.
    • The International Drilling segment saw a 9% increase in operating revenues, driven by a 5% increase in average rigs working and improved pricing.
    • The Drilling Solutions segment reported a 23% increase in operating revenues, largely due to the acquisition of Parker operations.
    • The Rig Technologies segment experienced a 12% decrease in operating revenues, attributed to a decline in U.S. activity.
  • Geographical Performance:
    • U.S. drilling activity decreased, reflecting a reduction in rig counts due to market volatility and reduced capital availability.
    • International markets showed an expansion in production capacity and increased drilling activity, particularly in unconventional resources.
  • Sales Units:
    • The average number of rigs working in the U.S. decreased by 10.5 rigs, or 13%, while internationally, the average increased by 4 rigs, or 5%, indicating a shift in operational focus.
  • Future Outlook:
    • The demand for services and products is closely tied to oil and gas prices, which are influenced by supply and demand cycles and geopolitical uncertainties.
    • The company anticipates that recent reductions in U.S. interest rates may positively impact future rig demand.
    • Internationally, an increase in oilfield activity is expected due to expanded production capacity and unconventional resource development.
  • Recent Developments:
    • The acquisition of Parker Drilling Company was completed on March 11, 2025, contributing to increased revenues in the Drilling Solutions segment and impacting general and administrative expenses due to integration costs.

SEC Filing: NABORS INDUSTRIES LTD [ NBR ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: JUNIPER NETWORKS INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Juniper Networks Inc., a leading provider of networking solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial and operational performance, driven by increased sales across all customer solutions and verticals. The company has also made strides in operational efficiency and cost management, contributing to a return to profitability.

Financial Highlights

Total Net Revenues: $1,280.2 million, an 11% increase compared to the same period in 2024, driven by higher sales volume across all customer solutions and verticals.

Gross Margin: $754.7 million, representing 59.0% of net revenues, slightly down from 59.3% in 2024 due to an unfavorable revenue mix, partially offset by higher service revenue and lower inventory-related expenses.

Operating Income: $89.4 million, a significant improvement from an operating loss of $14.2 million in 2024, primarily due to lower share-based compensation and merger-related charges.

Net Income: $64.1 million, compared to a net loss of $0.8 million in 2024, reflecting improved operational efficiency and cost management.

Net Income Per Share: Basic and Diluted EPS of $0.19, compared to $0.00 in 2024, indicating a return to profitability.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company reported an increase in net revenues across all customer solutions, with Wide Area Networking showing a 16% increase, Campus and Branch up by 22%, and Data Center growing by 9%. Hardware Maintenance and Professional Services also saw a 2% increase.

Geographical Performance: The Americas region, particularly the United States, experienced a significant 24% increase in net revenues. However, the EMEA region saw a decline of 7%, while the APAC region experienced a modest 5% growth.

Customer Segments: Revenue from the Cloud vertical increased by 29%, while the Enterprise segment grew by 12%. The Service Provider segment remained relatively flat with a slight decrease.

Software and Security Products: Software and related services revenue increased by 13%, driven by strong sales of software support and maintenance and SaaS contracts. Total security offerings saw a marginal increase of 1%.

Operational Efficiency: The company undertook efforts to improve product gross margins through performance and quality improvements, supply chain optimization, pricing management, and increased software and solution sales.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates that global economic conditions and uncertainties regarding tariffs may impact overall performance starting in the second quarter of 2025. Management is actively monitoring these factors to mitigate potential impacts on operations.

SEC Filing: JUNIPER NETWORKS INC [ JNPR ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: GREENWAY TECHNOLOGIES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Greenway Technologies Inc., a company specializing in the development of proprietary gas-to-liquids (GTL) synthesis gas conversion systems, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and key business activities, reflecting its ongoing efforts in research and development, as well as strategic market positioning.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $0, The company reported no revenues for the quarters ended March 31, 2025, and 2024.
  • Net Loss: $683,641, The net loss increased by $326,570 compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to increases in research and development expenses, legal expenses, consulting fees, travel expenses, and commission expenses.
  • Loss per share - basic and diluted: $(0.00), The loss per share remained unchanged from the previous year, despite the increase in net loss.
  • Operating Expenses: $532,142, Operating expenses increased significantly due to higher general and administrative expenses and research and development costs.
  • Interest Expense: $151,499, Interest expense decreased slightly due to an adjustment in interest payable on one note payable.

Business Highlights

  • GTL Technology Development: Greenway Technologies, Inc. is focused on the research and development of proprietary gas-to-liquids (GTL) synthesis gas conversion systems. The company has developed the G-Reformer, a key component in converting natural gas into syngas, which is then used to produce fuels and chemicals. The company aims to become a significant producer of renewable GTL synthesized diesel and jet fuels, with a focus on U.S. market opportunities.
  • Patent and Licensing Agreements: The company holds several patents related to its GTL technology, including U.S. Patent 10,633,594 B1 for syngas generation. Additionally, Greenway Technologies has an exclusive worldwide patent licensing agreement with the University of Texas at Arlington for its natural gas reforming technologies.
  • Research and Development Activities: During the first quarter of 2025, the company expanded its research and development efforts, including an enhanced relationship with the University of Texas at Arlington and the purchase of a G-Reformer unit for $50,000.
  • Operational Strategy: The company is focused on executing business operations more fully in 2025 and exploring strategic and partnership opportunities to enhance its market presence and operational capabilities.
  • Market Position and Competition: Greenway Technologies operates in the GTL industry, which converts natural gas into high-quality liquid products. The company differentiates itself with its scalable and transportable GTL plants, designed to meet specific gas field production requirements. Key competitors include major industry players like Shell and Sasol, but Greenway's technology is tailored for smaller, distributed applications.
  • Future Outlook: Management plans to raise additional funds through public or private offerings to support ongoing operations and further implement the business plan. The company is also focused on generating revenues from its GTL technology and expanding its market presence.
  • Employee Structure: As of the filing date, the company has three employees, with two under employment agreements. The company considers its employee relations to be satisfactory.

SEC Filing: GREENWAY TECHNOLOGIES INC [ GWTI ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: CB Financial Services, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
CB Financial Services, Inc., a regional banking institution, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter, providing a detailed overview of its financial and operational performance. The report highlights the company's stable interest income, despite market fluctuations, and outlines strategic shifts in its business operations aimed at enhancing future profitability.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest and Dividend Income: $17.847 million, reflecting a slight decrease from the previous year, indicating stable interest income despite market fluctuations.
  • Net Interest and Dividend Income: $11.311 million, showing a decrease compared to the previous year, highlighting the impact of increased interest expenses.
  • Net Income: $1.909 million, a significant decrease from the previous year's $4.196 million, primarily due to increased noninterest expenses and lower noninterest income.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share (EPS): $0.37, down from $0.82 in the previous year, reflecting the decrease in net income.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.35, compared to $0.82 in the previous year, indicating a reduction in earnings available to shareholders.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company derives its income primarily from banking and bank-related services, including interest income on commercial, commercial mortgage, residential real estate, and consumer loan financing, as well as interest and dividend income on securities, insurance commissions, and fees generated from deposit services.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates nine offices in Greene, Allegheny, Washington, Fayette, and Westmoreland Counties in southwestern Pennsylvania, and three offices in Marshall and Ohio Counties in West Virginia, indicating a strong regional presence in these areas.
  • Sales Units: Loan production totaled $28.6 million, with $15.6 million of loans paid off since December 31, 2024, reflecting active lending operations.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue focusing on more profitable commercial products, as indicated by the discontinuation of indirect automobile loan production. This strategic shift is expected to impact future loan portfolio composition and profitability.

SEC Filing: CB Financial Services, Inc. [ CBFV ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: Ferrovial SE expected to post earnings of 17 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Ferrovial SE is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 13 for the period ending March 31 2025

  • The Amsterdam Noord-holland-based company is expected to report a 64.5% decrease in revenue to €667.333 million from €1.88 billion a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 6 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Ferrovial SE is for earnings of 17 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 15 "strong buy" or "buy," 5 "hold" and 2 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 0.9% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Ferrovial SE is €45.60​, below​ its last closing price of $48.92. ​​​

data.title: NATO's Rutte wants 2032 deadline for new defence spending goals, Dutch PM says
data.description:
NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte has proposed that alliance members meet higher targets for defence spending by 2032, Dutch Prime Minister Dick Schoof said on Friday.

Reuters reported last week that Rutte had proposed NATO members raise defence spending to 3.5% of their GDP, and a further 1.5% on broader security-related items to meet U.S. President Donald Trump's demand for a 5% target.

Speaking to reporters after a cabinet meeting, Schoof confirmed those figures, which would represent a major increase from the current NATO goal of spending 2% of GDP on defence.

NATO aims to agree the new targets at a summit of alliance leaders including Trump, in The Hague, on June 24-25.

"Rutte has sent a letter to all NATO members to say that he expects that the commitment at the NATO summit will be 3.5% on hard military spending, to be reached in 2032, and 1.5% on related spending such as on infrastructure, cyber security, and similar things also to be reached by 2032," Schoof said.

NATO did not immediately respond to a request for comment on Schoof's remarks.

He said the government of the Netherlands, where Rutte was a long-serving prime minister, would continue discussions to determine its position on the proposal.

Other NATO members were also considering Rutte's request and would have to discuss it with their parliaments, he added.

Twenty-two of NATO's 32 members meet the current 2% target. But leaders across NATO say that goal is no longer sufficient as they now regard Russia as a far greater threat in the wake of its 2022 full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

No NATO country meets Trump's 5% target. According to NATO, the United States spends about 3.2% of GDP on defence. Poland spends the biggest share of its GDP - at over 4%.

data.title: Sector Update: Health Care
data.description:
Health care stocks declined Friday afternoon, with the NYSE Health Care Index and the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV) each decreasing 0.4%.

The iShares Biotechnology ETF (IBB) retreated 1.2%.

In corporate news, Apellis Pharmaceuticals shares were falling 3%. The company has had a difficult Q1 as charitable foundation funding gaps and weaker-than-expected Syfovre sales weigh on the outlook, BofA Securities said in a Friday report. BofA downgraded the stock to neutral from buy and cut the price target to $23 from $41.

data.title: Sector Update: Tech
data.description:
Tech stocks were mixed Friday afternoon, with the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) decreasing 0.2% and the SPDR S&P Semiconductor ETF (XSD) adding 1.1%.

The Philadelphia Semiconductor index rose 0.7%.

In sector news, tech industry representatives are warning that new US investments pledged by major firms could be put at risk if Congress doesn't fully reinstate a tax deduction for research and development, Bloomberg reported, citing the Information Technology Industry Council.

In corporate news, Nvidia-backed CoreWeave is in talks to raise $1.5 billion in debt after its IPO raised less than what was targeted, Bloomberg reported. Separately, Nvidia plans to release a reduced-performance version of its H20 AI chip for China by July, following US export restrictions on the original model, Reuters reported. Nvidia shares were shedding 0.8% and CoreWeave fell past 7%.

data.title: GCM Grosvenor Is Maintained at Outperform by Oppenheimer
Sector Update: Consumer
data.description:
Consumer stocks were mixed Friday afternoon, with the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLP) decreasing 0.5% and the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) up 0.2%.

In corporate news, Lyft shares jumped 24%. The company unexpectedly swung to a Q1 profit amid double-digit gains in gross bookings, while it also forecast continued growth for the key metric in the ongoing three-month period.
